task_completion.visual_evidence: The GCompris application main menu or interface is visible on the screen.
A screenshot shows the agent playing or completing an activity in the Math/Numbers category (e.g., counting, arithmetic) within GCompris.

task_completion.description: Complete a Language and Literacy activity
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot shows the agent playing or completing an activity in the Language/ABC category (e.g., letters, reading, typing) within GCompris.
A screenshot shows the agent playing or completing an activity in the Science/Experiment category within GCompris.
A screenshot shows the agent playing or completing a game in the Spatial/Logical Thinking category (e.g., puzzles, mazes, strategy games) within GCompris.

task_completion.description: Create the developmental assessment battery report file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor is open, showing a file named 'developmental_assessment_battery.txt' being saved to or edited in the ~/Desktop directory.

task_completion.description: Document findings for all four domains in the report
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text editor shows the report containing separate sections for Mathematical Reasoning, Language and Literacy, Scientific Inquiry, and Spatial/Logical Thinking. Each section includes an activity name, cognitive domain, developmental skill tested, and performance details. The text appears to be substantial (at least 500 bytes).

integrity.description: Agent actually interacted with the GCompris GUI
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the agent navigating the GCompris interface and actively playing the activities, rather than just writing a report from memory.

integrity.description: Agent did not bypass the task using terminal scripts
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should not just use 'echo' or a script to generate the text file without opening GCompris.
The report should contain specific activity names and performance details that plausibly match the activities seen in the GCompris gameplay screenshots.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The report must be saved to /home/ga/Desktop/developmental_assessment_battery.txt. The four required domains correspond to GCompris categories: Mathematical Reasoning (Math/Numbers), Language and Literacy (Language/ABC), Scientific Inquiry (Science/Experiment), and Spatial/Logical Thinking (Games). The final report must be at least 500 bytes and contain documented findings from all four domains.
